source.title: Uncertainty-based competition between prefrontal and dorsolateral striatal systems for behavioral control

confidence_rationale: Nat Neurosci paper, foundational computational and theoretical synthesis: PFC implements a model-based (goal-directed, flexible, statistically efficient) controller; dorsolateral striatum implements a model-free (habitual, computationally cheap, retrospective) controller; the brain arbitrates between them by uncertainty. This is the canonical biological decomposition of action representation into TWO computationally distinct systems, with substrate-level grounding. Confidence 0.85 because the framework is canonical and the substrate-level evidence (lesion, fMRI, behavioural) has accumulated robustly in the 20 years since.
failure_signatures: The two-system framework is a coarse decomposition. More recent literature (Cushman 2013; Dolan & Dayan 2013, this review) argues for a spectrum or for additional intermediate levels. An REE architectural commitment that maps cleanly to 'two and only two systems' would over-simplify the biology.

The paper does not address the option / hierarchical-RL level (Botvinick 2009, this review). The 'model-based' system as Daw et al. describe it is flat goal-directed planning, not hierarchical option composition. REE's decomposition needs both -- the goal-directed/habit dichotomy AND the hierarchical-options level.

Uncertainty-based arbitration is the proposed mechanism but the precise neural substrate of the arbitrator is not pinned down. ACC is one candidate; the SD-032b dACC analog in REE is functionally close but not identical.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The brain contains multiple systems for behavioral choice, including one associated with prefrontal cortex (model-based, goal-directed, statistically efficient at the cost of computational complexity) and another with dorsolateral striatum (model-free, habitual, computationally cheap but inflexible). A Bayesian arbitrator deploys each controller when it should be most accurate, based on the relative uncertainty of their predictions.
mapping.ree_translation: For SD-004 (action-object space, action_object_decoder), ARC-021 (three BG-like cortico-striatal loops with distinct learning channels), and MECH-292 (ranked ghost-goal bank): the canonical biological action decomposition is at minimum TWO levels (model-free habit cache + model-based goal-directed planner). REE V3 maps onto the model-based side: HippocampalModule.propose_trajectories with E2 transition model + E3 CEM scoring + MECH-293 ghost probes is functionally a goal-directed planner. There is NO V3 analogue of the model-free habit-cache substrate -- specifically, no cached state-action value table updated by experience that can produce action selection without rollout. This is a real architectural lacuna for V3 if V3 wants to express habit-like automaticity, monostrategy lock-in via cached habits, or model-free vs model-based competition.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The Daw 2005 framework is a flat two-system decomposition. REE's ARC-021 already specifies three BG-like loops with distinct learning channels, which is a richer architecture than the Daw 2005 two-system model. So adding a habit-cache substrate to REE is not just adding the model-free system from Daw -- it is filling in the cached-action-value role within ARC-021's three-loop frame. Also: the Daw 2005 'PFC' is a heterogeneous region; REE's frontal substrate is partitioned into SD-033a/b/c/d, so the model-based-PFC mapping is distributed across REE substrates rather than localised.
mapping.source_context: Computational and theoretical synthesis paper. Builds on prior empirical work (rodent lesion studies of DLS / PFC, human fMRI of devaluation transfer paradigms) to propose a normative Bayesian arbitration framework. The specific paper is theoretical; the empirical anchor work is reviewed and integrated.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality very high (Nat Neurosci, foundational, heavily cited, framework remains canonical 20 years later). Mapping fidelity high: the model-based / model-free dichotomy maps cleanly onto REE's existing planner (model-based) and the missing habit substrate. Transfer risk low because the framework is computational/theoretical and translates directly to REE's architectural scaffolding.
